current_implemented_build: V60.3.20 Layer 1 Visual Consistency and TrustMap Stack Consolidation
patch_type: TrustMap visual stack consolidation after V60.3.19
release_chain_status: V60.3.20 is the current implemented build. It consolidates the previous Layer 1 neutral-state and view-mode recovery behavior into one debounced module instead of stacking additional overlay layers.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3.20. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.

trustmap_visual_stack.v60_3_12: PNG asset mapping and interaction recovery
trustmap_visual_stack.v60_3_13: Stoplight trust color and PNG path recovery
trustmap_visual_stack.v60_3_14: Connector trust-state preservation and chain loader
trustmap_visual_stack.v60_3_16: Centerline fiber connector overlay and three-pane separation
trustmap_visual_stack.v60_3_16_1: Trust Kernel right-panel detail and stoplight-only risk rows
trustmap_visual_stack.v60_3_20: Consolidated Layer 1 visual consistency and view-mode recovery

retired_runtime_layers.v60_3_18: Remains in repository for audit history but is no longer imported at runtime through the V60.3.14 chain.
retired_runtime_layers.v60_3_19: Remains in repository for audit history but is no longer imported at runtime through the V60.3.14 chain.

v60_3_20_scope_rule: V60.3.20 owns one debounced recovery layer for Layer 1 neutral brightness, uniform cube size, Third Parties sizing, Trust Kernel visibility, and post-view-mode visual recovery. It does not create a new movement system.
non_negotiable_trustmap_scope: Do not replace the TrustMap page
Do not remove the left Operational Trust Score panel
Do not remove the right-side score/detail area
Do not remove Layer 2 or Layer 3
Do not replace the radar / constellation environment
Do not override #v554World transform with CSS
Do not create a second movement system
Only map rendered PNG assets into the existing TrustMap core and Layer 1 slots
Trust-state colors must be stoplight green, yellow, or red only
Do not make the Briefing snapshot replace the full TrustMap page
layer1_rules: Normal state uses the native hologram cube with no red/yellow/green image glow.
Hover/focus shows a stoplight oval glow.
Click/select keeps the stoplight oval glow until another asset is selected.
All Layer 1 holographic cubes must render at the same apparent size.
Third Parties & Vendors must not be smaller, cropped, zoomed, square-boxed, or styled differently from the others.
Layer 1 assets should appear bright and solid unless explicitly changed.

stoplight_trust_color_rule.principle: Blue is neutral brand/system/fiber/AI-working state, not trust-state severity.

connector_rule: Default connectors should resemble white-blue fiber optic trust lines, draw center-to-center behind rendered objects, and allow selected/path highlights in stoplight green, yellow, or red.
three_pane_layout_rule: Left = Operational Trust Score and executive trust context. Center = Enterprise Trust Map. Right = selected item breakdown. All panes share top and bottom alignment and the right pane must not overlap the center pane.
briefing_snapshot_rule: The Briefing page TrustMap Snapshot uses assets/The Trust Map.png as a static executive preview. The full interactive TrustMap remains on the TrustMap tab.
information_architecture_rule: Evidence tab owns the full Manual Evidence Workbench. Runtime tab owns compact evidence requirements for action. Proof Pack tab owns proof-oriented evidence trace and defensibility context. Settings owns build/version/governance/prototype metadata.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative until validated through test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
v60_3_20_changes: Updates src/ui/v60-3-14-trustmap-background-oval-highlight-spacing.js to import V60.3.20 instead of V60.3.18 and V60.3.19
Consolidates Layer 1 neutral visual consistency and view-mode recovery into one debounced module
Forces the CyberShield Trust Kernel PNG and Layer 1 PNGs back into the TrustMap when older visuals return
Forces all Layer 1 PNGs, including Third Parties & Vendors, to the same apparent cube size
Removes normal-state image-level trust glow while preserving stoplight oval hover/focus/selected rings
Reduces redundant event listeners from the previous stacked visual recovery layers
prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It does not perform live enterprise integrations, live retrieval, live scoring, statistical validation, backend persistence, workflow automation, production enforcement, certification, or compliance determination.
qa_priority: Hard refresh the V60.3.20 test URL
Verify Briefing snapshot uses assets/The Trust Map.png
Open TrustMap and verify the CyberShield Trust Kernel appears immediately and stays visible
Verify all eight PNGs load from assets or accepted fallback paths
Verify all seven Layer 1 assets are evenly distributed and the same apparent cube size
Verify Third Parties & Vendors is no longer square-boxed or visually different
Verify CMMC, Devices & Endpoints, and Cloud & Infrastructure are not faded compared to other assets
Verify no constant red/yellow/green glow in normal Layer 1 state
Verify old glow does not return after TrustMap view controls
Verify map drag, wheel zoom, buttons, sliders, and view modes still work
Verify map does not feel slow or jittery
